title: Redesign: Ship Native Chameleon token set and ThemeBridge site-theme detection
label: design-tokens
description:
## Context
Lasso currently themes via `prefers-color-scheme` (src/ui/styles.css:24–33), but X has three site themes (Default, Dim #15202b, Lights Out #000) chosen independently of the OS — guaranteed mismatch for Dim and cross-theme users. The winning redesign direction pegs every token to a measured x.com value and detects the theme from the page itself.

## Scope
- Replace the `@theme` block in `src/ui/styles.css` wholesale with the Native Chameleon token set from the Redesign Brief (surfaces, ink/muted, hairlines, accent family incl. `--color-accent-text` and `--color-accent-strong`, status colors, X type scale 20/15/13/11, radii pill/16/12/4, `--shadow-menu` halo, motion tokens). Delete the `prefers-color-scheme` block entirely.
- Add `:host([data-x-theme="dim"])` and `:host([data-x-theme="lightsout"])` override blocks (literal selectors — the mount.tsx `:root`→`:host` rewrite is untouched).
- Add `:host { all: initial; font: 400 var(--text-body)/1.25rem var(--font-sans); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; color: var(--color-ink); }` hardening (host-page custom properties pierce the shadow boundary). Prepend `"TwitterChirp"` to `--font-sans`.
- New `src/ui/theme-bridge.ts`: detect via `getComputedStyle(document.body).backgroundColor` — exact map rgb(255,255,255)→light, rgb(21,32,43)→dim, rgb(0,0,0)→lightsout; fallback by relative luminance (≥0.6 light, ≥0.05 dim, else lightsout). MutationObserver on `document.body` with `attributeFilter:['style']`; module-level host registry; `registerThemedHost(host)` called inside `attachShadowRoot` so every per-tweet overlay host re-themes.
- Off-x.com (options page) fall back to `prefers-color-scheme`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Switching X's display setting between Default/Dim/Lights Out re-themes every Lasso surface (picker, bar, toasts, all overlay hosts) without reload.
- [ ] OS theme has no effect while on x.com.
- [ ] Lasso text renders in Chirp inside the shadow root on x.com.
- [ ] `:host { all: initial }` present; an X `:root` custom property cannot leak into Lasso styles.
- [ ] Side-by-side spot check on all three themes: Lasso surface/ink/hairline values match X's computed styles (1-hour calibration pass logged as a checklist in the PR).

title: Redesign: Build AnchoredPopover positioning primitive with focus and Escape handling
label: component
description:
## Context
The ListPicker floats detached at bottom-center pinned by its bottom edge (src/content/app.tsx:81), so the input jumps while typing and the surface is anchored to nothing. The redesign anchors the picker exactly where X's own caret menu opens.

## Scope
New `src/ui/AnchoredPopover.tsx`:
- Transparent click-catcher backdrop `fixed inset-0 z-[2147483645]` — click dismisses; must NOT block wheel scroll of the feed.
- Panel: `position:fixed`, `z-[2147483646]`, bg-surface, `--radius-dialog` 16px, `--shadow-menu`, 1px `--color-line` border on dim/lightsout.
- Props `{ anchorRect: () => DOMRect | null, align: 'caret' | 'above-center' }`. `caret`: panel top-RIGHT at `(anchor.right, anchor.bottom + 4px)`; anchor = `article.querySelector('[data-testid="caret"]')`, fallback article top-right; flip above when `viewportHeight − anchor.bottom < 440px`. `above-center`: bottom-center 8px above the invoking element. Clamp 16px to all viewport edges.
- One rAF loop while open: read `anchorRect()`, write `transform: translate3d(...)`. If the anchor is detached (X's virtualizer) or scrolled >40px out of viewport: freeze at last clamped position — never auto-dismiss.
- 0ms entrance/exit, both open paths.
- Focus: rAF → `input.focus({preventScroll:true})` on mount; restore the `document.activeElement` captured at open on unmount. Document-level capture keydown: Escape always dismisses; Tab/Shift+Tab trapped to the single focusable input.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Alt+L on a focused tweet opens the panel at the tweet's caret corner; near the viewport bottom it flips above with the panel's bottom edge pinned.
- [ ] Feed scrolls under the open popover (wheel not blocked); one click outside dismisses and is swallowed.
- [ ] Scrolling the anchor far away freezes the panel instead of dismissing; typing continues to work.
- [ ] Escape dismisses regardless of focus location; focus returns to the pre-open element.
- [ ] Exactly one `getBoundingClientRect` per frame, only while open (verify in profiler).
- [ ] Watch-item documented in code: if the freeze fallback reads as detached in user testing, switch to the ratified fixed top-anchored fallback (top: 96px centered, fixed-height body).

title: Redesign: Rebuild ListPicker as an anchored five-state combobox
label: component
description:
## Context
The current picker (src/ui/ListPicker.tsx) renders name-only rows, blocks every open on a forced network fetch (app.tsx:54 `force:true`), collapses logged-out/rate-limit/true-empty into a lying 'No matching lists' (app.tsx:56–58), never scrolls the active row into view, and uses an invisible 3%-lightness active state.

## Scope
Rewrite ListPicker inside AnchoredPopover per the Redesign Brief:
- 340px wide; Header 44px / Input 48px / Body / Footer 36px. Anti-jump: below-anchor body shrink-wraps to min(content, 364px); flipped-above body locked at 280px for the life of the open. (Fallback: fixed-height body in both orientations.)
- Header: `Add @jane to a List` / `Add 3 people to a List`. Input: magnifier icon, placeholder `Search Lists`, full combobox ARIA.
- Rows: full-bleed square, 56px, two lines — name with fuzzy-matched chars in `<b>` + lock icon for private Lists; `1,204 members` meta tabular-nums. Right slot: accent check when the selected author is already a member; return-arrow glyph on the keyboard-active row. Hover `--color-hover`; active `--color-accent-soft`; `scrollIntoView({block:'nearest'})` effect on activeIndex change. Groups: `Recent` (top 3 by listUsage) then `All Lists` on empty query.
- State machine in app.tsx: `idle/loading/error/empty/ready`. Open instantly from `listCache.lists()` (drop `force:true`), revalidate in background, reconcile. Cold cache: 3 skeleton rows (pulse 1.2s; static 0.6 under reduced-motion).
- Error: `Couldn't load your Lists` + reason (`You may be logged out of X` / `X rate limited Lasso — try again in a few minutes`) + `Retry` pill; `r` key retries. True-empty: `You don't have any Lists yet` / `Lists let you group people on X` + `Create a List on X` → x.com/i/lists/create. No-match: `No Lists match "des"` + `Clear search` + `Create "des" on X`.
- Footer: kbd legend `↑↓ Navigate · Enter Add · Esc Dismiss` + `3 selected` right-aligned.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Warm cache: picker shell + rows render in the same frame as Alt+L (0ms, no spinner).
- [ ] All five states reachable and visually distinct; logged-out no longer shows 'No matching lists'.
- [ ] Arrowing through 20+ Lists keeps the active row in view; active row clearly visible on all three themes.
- [ ] Typing never moves the input vertically, in both below and flipped placements.
- [ ] `r` retries from the error state without touching the mouse.
- [ ] Already-member rows show the accent check for single-author selections.

title: Redesign: Rebuild ActionBar with facepile, select-mode state, and selection-preserving progress
label: component
description:
## Context
The bar shows a bare count (avatarUrl is captured and used nowhere), 's' toggles a signal nothing reads (main.tsx:103–105), and `pick()` clears the selection before the assign run resolves (app.tsx:67–75) — making failures and rate limits unrecoverable.

## Scope
Rewrite `src/ui/ActionBar.tsx` + BottomStack in app.tsx per the Redesign Brief:
- BottomStack: one fixed wrapper (bottom 24px, centered, column-reverse, gap 8px, pointer-events none) containing ActionBar + ToastStack; kill the bottom-6/20/24 magic offsets.
- Bar 52px pill, bg-surface, shadow-menu, dark-theme hairline. Facepile (≤3 × 26px avatars, −8px overlap, surface rings, `+4` overflow) → click opens a review popover (AnchoredPopover above-center, 280px; 44px rows: avatar, @handle, display name, ✕ remove). Count `3 people selected` (number 700 tabular-nums). Hairline divider. Primary `Add to List` (X small-button: 32px pill, bg-accent, white 700; `active:scale-[.96]`; keycap chip `Alt+L` during the onboarding window). Clear ✕ icon-button, `aria-label="Clear selection"`.
- Select-mode state: when `selection.selectMode` is true, render at count 0 with crosshair glyph + `Select mode · click posts or press x · s when done` + `Done` pill (exits mode, keeps selection). Mirror mode as `data-mode="select"` on every overlay host.
- Progress state: `pick()` must NOT clear the selection. Swap to spinner + `Adding 2 of 5 to Design Folks…` with per-author increments + `Stop` pill (aborts remaining calls; un-attempted authors stay selected; bar shows `2 added · 3 still selected`). Clear only the successfully-added subset after results return. Partial failure: bar persists with `2 couldn't be added` (danger) + `Retry failed`.
- Motion: enter translateY(8px)+opacity 150ms ease-swift; exit 120ms reverse; transform/opacity only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Facepile renders real avatars; review popover can remove individuals from the selection.
- [ ] Pressing `s` visibly changes the UI (bar appears at count 0; overlays reveal — dependent on the TweetOverlay issue).
- [ ] A rate-limit stop mid-run leaves the remainder selected and the bar up; Stop aborts and reports the split.
- [ ] `selection.clear()` is never called before results return; only added authors are removed from the selection.
- [ ] Bar enter/exit animates; no animation when the select-mode state toggles via keyboard (0ms).

title: Redesign: Rebuild Toast into a Sonner-mechanics ToastStack with outcome variants and scoped undo
label: component
description:
## Context
One cheerful blue pill currently renders every outcome — failures and rate limits look identical to success, white-on-#1d9bf0 fails AA at 13px, the 4000ms timer is never cleared (app.tsx:74 wipes the next run's toast), quick actions are silent, and the live region mounts with text already present (often never announced).

## Scope
Rewrite `src/ui/Toast.tsx` as a stack manager (top of BottomStack) per the Redesign Brief:
- Card: surface-colored (the one sanctioned deviation), 48px min, ≤420px, radius 12px, shadow-menu, leading 20px status icon (success #00ba7c circle-check / warn triangle / danger alert-circle / info ⓘ), line 1 body ink, line 2 meta muted, trailing text-actions in `--color-accent-text`, hover-revealed ✕.
- Variants (exact strings): SUCCESS `Added 3 to Design Folks` / `1 was already in the List` / `View List` + `Undo` (kbd `Z`), 4s. PARTIAL `Added 2 to Design Folks · 1 failed` / `@handle is protected and can't be added` / `Retry`, 8s. RATE LIMIT (danger, no auto-dismiss) `X rate limit reached` / `Added 4 · 6 still selected — try again in 12 min` (from `x-rate-limit-reset`). QUICK-ACTION `Muted @jane` / `Undo` for Alt+M/Alt+N/block (replaces console-only paths in main.tsx:124–135); failure `Couldn't mute @jane` / `Retry`. NUDGE `Hover a post first — or press j to focus one` (replaces console.warn main.tsx:109), 3s. TIP variants for onboarding (see FirstRun issue).
- Undo guard: assign-undo issues member-remove calls ONLY for `outcome === 'added'` results — never pre-existing memberships. `Z` triggers undo within a 10s post-toast window, then unbinds.
- Mechanics: enter translateY(16px)→0 + opacity 220ms ease-swift (CSS transition, interruptible); exit 150ms; stack max 3 with scale(1−0.05i)/−8i px; hover expands and pauses timers; timers per-toast, cleared on replace, paused on `document.hidden`.
- A11y: one persistent visually-hidden `<output aria-live="polite" role="status">` mounted at app start; swap `textContent` per event. Reduced-motion: opacity-only 100ms.
- Update `src/core/result-summary.ts` strings: kill `N not allowed` → per-author protected reason; `Nothing to add` → `Nothing was added` framing with reason.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each variant renders with the exact canonical string and correct icon/persistence; rate-limit toast never auto-dismisses.
- [ ] Two back-to-back assign runs: the second toast is never wiped by the first run's timer.
- [ ] Undo after a run that included already-member authors removes only the newly-added ones (unit test).
- [ ] `Z` undoes within 10s of the toast; after 10s the key does nothing.
- [ ] Screen reader announces every toast via the persistent live region.
- [ ] All toast text passes 4.5:1 on all three themes.

title: Redesign: Relocate TweetOverlay to the avatar corner with hover/select-mode visibility and selector-health notice
label: component
description:
## Context
Overlays are prepended inside `[data-testid="User-Name"]` on every tweet (main.tsx:31–40), permanently shifting X's author row and adding always-on chrome noise. The ✓ is a font-dependent text glyph; unselected circles have no hover affordance; select mode gates nothing. Separately, selector breakage is invisible (scanner silently finds nothing).

## Scope
- Injection (main.tsx): append the shadow host into the tweet's avatar container (`[data-testid="Tweet-User-Avatar"]`, position:relative anchor), absolutely positioned right:-2px bottom:-2px, z-index 2. Fallback if the container can't anchor: keep User-Name placement but gate it to select mode only.
- Visual (TweetOverlay.tsx): 22px circle. Unselected: bg-surface, 1.5px `--color-line-strong` border. Hover: accent border + 40%-opacity accent check preview. Selected: bg accent, 2px surface outer ring, white inline SVG check (path `M20 6L9 17l-5-5`, stroke 2.5, round caps). Press scale .92 @120ms.
- Visibility: host carries `data-mode`/`data-hovered`; default opacity 0 + pointer-events none; revealed on `[data-hovered]`, `[data-mode="select"]`, or `.selected`; opacity transition 120ms. Wire the existing mousemove tracker (main.tsx:80–93) to set/clear `data-hovered` — no new listeners. Keyboard `x`/Alt+L flip state in 0ms.
- Hit area 40px via `before:-inset-2`; `aria-pressed`; labels `Select @jane` / `Remove @jane from selection`.
- Selector health: in tweet-scanner, if >200 mutations observed with 0 tweet matches in a session, fire the one-time info toast `Lasso can't read the timeline — X may have changed. Check for an update.`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Zero layout shift on X's author row (compare DOM before/after injection).
- [ ] Timeline shows no Lasso chrome until hover, select mode, or an existing selection.
- [ ] Selected checks stay visible while scrolling; check ring meets 3:1 against avatar imagery.
- [ ] Check renders identically across platforms (SVG, not glyph).
- [ ] Simulated selector breakage (rename the testid in a fixture) produces the health toast exactly once.

title: Redesign: Wire the keyboard grammar — Alt+Shift+L direct add, Z undo, r retry, Esc precedence, select-mode body-click
label: component
description:
## Context
Judges' grafts from the losing directions, all keyboard-economy wins: the Default List has no chord, undo/retry are mouse-only, Esc semantics are undefined per-surface, and bulk pointer selection requires aiming at 22px circles.

## Scope
- `src/content/keyboard.ts`: add `{ combo: 'Alt+Shift+l', command: 'add-to-default-list' }` and `{ combo: '?', command: 'help' }` to DEFAULT_KEYMAP. `add-to-default-list`: if `settings.defaultListId` is set, assign the target author(s) directly (same progress/toast pipeline, no picker); if unset, fall through to the normal picker open with a one-time info toast `Set a default List in Lasso settings to skip this step`.
- Escape arbiter (document-level, capture): one deterministic stack — open dialog (help/welcome) → ListPicker → ActionBar review popover → select mode (exit, keep selection) → clear selection. Exactly one layer consumed per press. Remove all per-surface Escape special-casing.
- `Z`: bound only during the 10s post-toast undo window (see ToastStack issue); never a global binding.
- `r`: retries while the picker error state is shown (scoped to the open picker).
- Select-mode body-click: while `selection.selectMode` is true, a capture-phase click listener on tweet articles toggles selection and `preventDefault`s; removed when the mode exits.
- Platform-aware keycap rendering helper: `Alt` on Windows/Linux, `⌥` on macOS, detected once at mount; used by every kbd chip (ActionBar, picker footer, help sheet, popup).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] With a default List set, Alt+Shift+L on a hovered post adds the author and shows the success toast with no picker.
- [ ] Esc pressed repeatedly walks the stack in the documented order from any state; with nothing open it clears the selection.
- [ ] In select mode, clicking a tweet body toggles it without opening the tweet; outside select mode clicks behave natively.
- [ ] `Z`/`r` work only inside their scoped windows; neither fires while typing in X's composer (isTypingTarget respected).
- [ ] All keycap chips show Alt on the owner's Windows machine and ⌥ on macOS.

title: Story: Build the '?' ShortcutHelp overlay from the live keymap
label: onboarding
description:
## Context
The keymap is documented only in docs/; the single piece of user guidance is a console.warn. X itself opens a shortcuts modal on '?', so the chameleon move is to mirror it exactly — and the entire audience (X power users) already knows the key.

## Scope
New `src/ui/ShortcutHelp.tsx` per the Redesign Brief:
- Full-screen backdrop `--color-backdrop`; centered dialog ≤600px, ≤80vh, radius-dialog, bg-surface, shadow-menu. Header 53px: circular close button left, title `Keyboard shortcuts` (text-title), right corner: `Lasso` text-meta muted (one of the product's two visible wordmarks).
- Body grid `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(260px, 1fr))`. Sections: Selection (`x` Select author under cursor · `s` Select mode · `Alt+L` Add to a List · `Alt+Shift+L` Add straight to your default List), Quick actions (`Alt+M` Mute author · `Alt+N` Not interested), In the List picker (`↑ ↓` Navigate · `Enter` Add · `Esc` Dismiss), After a toast (`Z` Undo), Help (`?` This sheet). Rows 40px: label left, keycap chips right (20px, radius-key, elevated bg, hairline border) rendered from the LIVE keymap via `canonicalCombo` so future rebinds self-document.
- Footer: `j and k move between posts — those are X's own shortcuts. Lasso never overrides them.` + `Lasso settings` link (accent-text) opening the options page.
- Behavior: 0ms entrance/exit (keyboard-triggered); Esc/backdrop/close dismiss; focus trap (close button + settings link, wrapping); `role=dialog aria-modal=true aria-label="Keyboard shortcuts"`; focus restored on close.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `?` opens the sheet from the timeline (skipped while typing); `?` or Esc closes it.
- [ ] Rebinding a combo in the keymap table changes the rendered chip with no copy edits.
- [ ] Tab cycles only the two interactive elements; focus returns to the pre-open element on close.
- [ ] Visual parity with X's own shortcuts modal on all three themes (screenshot comparison).

title: Story: First-run welcome card and decaying coachmarks
label: onboarding
description:
## Context
onInstalled is a console.debug; users are dropped cold onto a timeline (and with hidden-by-default overlays, a user who never learns the gestures may never discover the product — the welcome card and decay hints carry that load). Superhuman's decaying-label pattern measurably lifted shortcut adoption (+20%).

## Scope
- `src/background/index.ts`: `onInstalled` (reason `install`) → `chrome.tabs.create({url:'https://x.com/home#lasso-welcome'})`; also `chrome.runtime.setUninstallURL(<one-question form>)`.
- WelcomeCard (content script renders when hash present or `lasso:onboarded` unset, after the timeline mounts): X dialog anatomy, 380px, backdrop. Title `Lasso is ready`; three rows: `Hover any post and press Alt+L to file its author into a List` / `Press s to select many people, then add them all at once` / `Press ? anytime to see every shortcut`; primary pill `Try select mode` (enters select mode + dismisses); text `Skip`; footer `Lasso runs entirely in your browser. Nothing leaves x.com.` Dismiss writes `lasso:onboarded=true` (storage.sync). Entrance 150ms opacity.
- Decay state in `chrome.storage.local` `lasso:coach = {installedAt, assigns, unitTooltipImpressions}`; active while `< 7 days AND assigns < 5`. While active: (a) `Alt+L` keycap beside the ActionBar CTA; (b) first pointer-driven assign → toast `Tip: Alt+L on a hovered post does this without the mouse`; (c) first overlay hover each session → X-anatomy tooltip `Select — then add everyone to a List at once`; (d) count hover tooltip `Lasso adds people to Lists, not posts.` (max 3 impressions). Every successful assign increments `assigns`.
- Moment-of-relevance nudge (independent of decay, once per install): after 3 individual selections in one session → toast `Tip: press s to select by clicking posts`.
- `Replay intro` action (wired from the Settings issue) resets `lasso:onboarded` + `lasso:coach`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Fresh install opens x.com and shows the card once; Esc/Skip/Got-it dismiss permanently; `Try select mode` enters the mode.
- [ ] All coachmarks vanish after 5 assigns (or a simulated 7-day clock) and the UI returns to zero hint chrome.
- [ ] Keyboard-driven assigns never trigger the Alt+L tip.
- [ ] The select-mode nudge fires exactly once, only after 3 individual selections in one session.

title: Story: Resolve the REST-vs-DOM backend default and realign PRD/CONTEXT/ADRs
label: settings
description:
## Context
`src/core/settings.ts:23` ships `DEFAULT_SETTINGS.backend = 'rest'` while docs/PRD.md, docs/CONTEXT.md, and the blueprint all tell a 'sanctioned DOM automation is the safe default; GraphQL is opt-in with a caveat' trust story — and the comment on the field itself still says 'DOM is the policy-conservative default (ADR-0001)'. All three design-review judges independently flagged this as a blocker: the settings page's backend-disclosure copy (next issue) cannot ship against a contradiction, or the trust copy lies in the exact place trust is built.

## Scope
- Make the product decision: which backend is the shipped default (rest or dom), and what is REST's stated policy position relative to the DOM-automation story.
- Align `DEFAULT_SETTINGS.backend` and its doc comment in `src/core/settings.ts` with the decision.
- Update docs/PRD.md (user story 5), docs/CONTEXT.md, docs/blueprint/2026-06-07-lasso-blueprint.md §7, and ADR-0001 (or supersede it with a new ADR) to tell one consistent story.
- Confirm the final radio-card copy for the three backends (Redesign Brief, Settings section) reflects the decided default — the word '(default)' must sit on the right card.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `DEFAULT_SETTINGS.backend`, its comment, PRD, CONTEXT, blueprint, and ADRs all name the same default.
- [ ] A new or amended ADR records the decision and the reasoning (including REST's policy position).
- [ ] The settings disclosure copy is signed off against the decided default before the options page issue merges.
- [ ] No user-facing string anywhere claims a default the code does not ship.

title: Story: Settings options page, toolbar icon, badge states, and action popup
label: settings
description:
## Context
A full typed settings store exists (src/core/settings.ts) but there is no options_ui, no popup, no icons in src/manifest.config.ts — every setting including activation (ADR-0006) is reachable only by hand-editing chrome.storage, the promised GraphQL disclosure has no surface, and the toolbar shows a generic placeholder with no dormant/wake feedback.

## Scope
- Manifest: add `options_ui {page:'src/options/index.html', open_in_tab:true}`, `icons` {16,32,48,128}, `action.default_icon`, and a 280px action popup. Icon: single SVG path — lasso rope whose open loop closes into a check, 1.5px stroke on a 20px grid, single-color (#0f1419 / auto dark variant); 16px variant drops the tail. No cowboy clipart.
- Options page (Preact, same token sheet, prefers-color-scheme fallback): 600px centered, X settings anatomy. Sections per the Redesign Brief: Activation radios; 'How Lasso talks to X' radio cards with the disclosure copy (BLOCKED on the backend-default issue); Default List select (`None — always ask`, description `Alt+Shift+L adds straight to this List`); Keyboard shortcuts read-only live table + `Press ? on x.com anytime`; Accessibility toggle `Higher-contrast buttons` (CTA fills swap to `--color-accent-strong`); Privacy & data card — `Lasso has no servers. Your X session, your Lists, and your usage stats never leave this browser.` + `Clear Lasso data` (wipes lasso:lists, lasso:list-usage, lasso:settings, lasso:coach; inline `Cleared`) + `Replay intro`. Footer: version + `Report an issue`.
- Toolbar behavior: dormant on-demand tabs get badge `zz` (#536471); waking fires toast `Lasso is awake on this tab` and clears it; while a selection is active the badge shows the live count. Popup: state line (`Active on x.com` / `Asleep — click to wake`), top-3 shortcuts as kbd rows, `All settings →`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every field of LassoSettings is editable from the options page and persists via storage.sync.
- [ ] On-demand mode: dormant tab shows `zz`; clicking wakes Lasso, fires the toast, clears the badge.
- [ ] Selecting 3 people shows `3` on the toolbar badge; clearing removes it.
- [ ] `Clear Lasso data` wipes exactly the four lasso:* keys and confirms inline.
- [ ] High-contrast toggle visibly swaps CTA fills and passes 4.5:1 (verify with a contrast checker).
- [ ] Icon renders crisply at 16/32/48/128 in light and dark toolbars.

title: Story: Canonical strings copy pass across all surfaces
label: copy
description:
## Context
Current copy is developer-flavored ('Filter your lists…', 'No matching lists', 'N not allowed', 'Nothing to add') and the product's central unit is ambiguous (checkmarks light per tweet, the bar counts authors, no string ever says which). The redesign fixes this structurally: every surface counts PEOPLE, X vocabulary throughout (List capitalized, posts not tweets), and the name 'Lasso' appears exactly twice on x.com.

## Scope
Implement the 20 canonical strings from the Product Story verbatim, replacing all legacy strings. Key file targets: `src/core/result-summary.ts` (outcome lines — kill 'N not allowed' and 'Nothing to add'), `src/ui/ListPicker.tsx` (header, placeholder, five state views, footer legend), `src/ui/ActionBar.tsx` (count, select-mode line, progress line, Stop/Done/Retry labels), `src/ui/Toast.tsx` (all variants), `src/content/main.tsx` (nudge toast replacing console.warn), FirstRun/help/settings surfaces (trust line, shortcuts footer). Add a single strings module (e.g. `src/ui/strings.ts`) so the canon lives in one place and the voice rules (sentence case, `·` separators, tabular numbers, people/posts/List vocabulary) are enforceable in review.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Grep finds zero instances of: 'Filter your lists', 'No matching lists', 'not allowed', 'Nothing to add', 'selected' without a 'people' unit on the bar.
- [ ] All 20 canonical strings appear verbatim and are sourced from the strings module.
- [ ] The string 'Lasso' is user-visible in exactly two places on x.com (help-sheet corner, help-sheet settings link) plus toolbar/options surfaces off-feed.
- [ ] Counts use the singular/plural pairs specified (`Add @jane to a List` / `Add 3 people to a List`; `1 person selected` / `3 people selected`).
- [ ] Failure copy never uses metaphor or exclamation; every failure names a cause and offers one verb.

title: Redesign: Motion conformance pass — entrances, exits, and reduced-motion
label: motion
description:
## Context
Motion is currently inconsistent: the ActionBar pops in/out with no transition while the toast has an entrance but no exit, and the strong ease-out token is barely used. The redesign defines a strict motion contract: keyboard-opened surfaces at 0ms always; pointer/system surfaces animate transform/opacity only, under 250ms, with the swift ease.

## Scope
Implement the Motion spec table from the Redesign Brief across all components:
- 0ms (no entrance, no exit): ListPicker (both open paths), ShortcutHelp, select-mode bar state, overlay check state flips from keyboard.
- Animated: ActionBar enter 150ms translateY(8px)+opacity / exit 120ms (mount persistently with a data-state attribute or exit-clone so the exit can actually run); Toast enter 220ms / exit 150ms via CSS transitions (interruptible, never keyframes); overlay hover reveal 120ms opacity; press scales 120ms; WelcomeCard 150ms opacity.
- Spinner: 2px accent arc 750ms linear; reduced-motion replacement: 3-dot opacity cycle.
- Verify every transition animates only transform/opacity (no layout, shadow, or filter transitions).
- prefers-reduced-motion: keep the global 0.01ms clamp; skeleton pulse → static 0.6 opacity; toasts → opacity-only 100ms.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Frame-by-frame check (devtools): Alt+L picker and `?` sheet appear with zero transitional frames.
- [ ] ActionBar visibly slides/fades in and out; no instant pop on clear.
- [ ] Toast enter is interruptible (a second toast retargets mid-flight without jumping).
- [ ] With reduced-motion enabled, no element moves; spinners become opacity cycles; nothing conveys state by motion alone.
- [ ] DevTools performance pass while scrolling with the bar visible: no layout thrash or non-composited animations from Lasso.

title: Redesign: A11y hardening — combobox ARIA, focus management, live region, contrast verification
label: a11y
description:
## Context
The audit found: Escape handled only on the picker input, no focus trap or restoration, fragile `autofocus` in shadow DOM, incomplete combobox ARIA (no aria-modal, no aria-activedescendant ids), an active-row affordance at 3% lightness, white-on-#1d9bf0 failing AA, no scrollIntoView for keyboard navigation, and a live region that mounts with text already present (frequently never announced).

## Scope
A verification-and-gap-closure pass across the rebuilt components (this issue owns the cross-cutting checklist; component issues own their local wiring):
- Combobox: input `role=combobox aria-expanded aria-controls="lasso-listbox" aria-activedescendant`; options with stable ids, `role=option`, `aria-selected`; `scrollIntoView({block:'nearest'})` on activeIndex change.
- Focus: explicit rAF focus on open (no `autofocus`); capture and restore the opener's focus on every dismissal path (Esc, outside click, pick, programmatic close); traps — single-focusable in the picker, wrapping trap in ShortcutHelp/WelcomeCard.
- Escape: document-level capture arbiter per the Esc grammar; works regardless of focus location.
- Live region: one persistent visually-hidden `<output aria-live="polite" role="status">` mounted at app start; all toasts, count changes, and progress updates flow through it by swapping textContent.
- Contrast: verify ink-on-surface ≥7:1 and accent-text ≥4.5:1 on all three themes; selected-check ring ≥3:1 over media; document the single knowing CTA deviation and verify the `Higher-contrast buttons` toggle resolves it.
- Semantics: `aria-pressed` + per-author labels on overlays; `role=region` on the bar; `role=dialog aria-modal=true` + labels on dialogs; keycap chips aria-hidden with the action named in adjacent text; 40px hit areas.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Full keyboard-only walkthrough: select, review/remove via popover, assign, undo, retry an error, exit select mode — without a mouse and without focus ever being lost.
- [ ] NVDA (Windows) announces: picker open with title, active option changes, assign progress, every toast variant.
- [ ] Escape dismisses from any focus position; focus lands back on the pre-open element every time.
- [ ] Automated axe scan of every Lasso surface returns no violations other than the documented CTA deviation (which disappears with the high-contrast setting on).
- [ ] Contrast table for all three themes committed alongside the tokens.
